headline: Echoblast Opens New Datacenter in Sterling VA
rendered_text:
**FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**

**Echoblast Opens EBL-VA1 Datacenter in Sterling, Virginia, Expanding GPU Capacity to Meet Surging AI Training Demand**

SAN FRANCISCO — December 8, 2026 — Echoblast, Inc., a leading AI neocloud platform providing GPU rental and managed training/inference services, today announced the opening of its new EBL-VA1 datacenter facility in Sterling, Virginia. The facility, developed in partnership with Equinix, brings 4 MW of initial GPU compute capacity online and represents a major milestone in Echoblast's geographic expansion strategy to serve enterprise customers across the Eastern United States.

The Sterling location marks Echoblast's third operational datacenter since its founding in January 2025 and reinforces the company's commitment to reducing latency and improving infrastructure accessibility for AI development teams on the East Coast. The facility will initially host hundreds of high-performance GPUs optimized for large-scale model training and real-time inference workloads.

"The demand for reliable, scalable GPU infrastructure has never been higher," said Will Coleman, CEO and co-founder of Echoblast. "EBL-VA1 puts us closer to our enterprise customers and academic partners in the Northeast corridor. We're not just adding capacity—we're building the distributed backbone that modern AI development requires. This datacenter will help our customers train larger models faster and deploy them with lower latency to end users."

The Sterling facility leverages Equinix's world-class colocation infrastructure, redundant power systems, and carrier-neutral interconnection capabilities. Echoblast selected the Northern Virginia location for its proximity to major financial services, government, and technology hubs, as well as its robust fiber connectivity and power availability.

The new datacenter is equipped with Echoblast's proprietary orchestration and resource-management software, enabling customers to seamlessly scale workloads across multiple geographic regions. The platform abstracts underlying hardware complexity, allowing teams to focus on model development rather than infrastructure management. Customers can provision GPUs on-demand, access managed training pipelines, and deploy inference endpoints through a unified dashboard.

"Northern Virginia is a strategic hub for enterprise AI adoption," said Angela Holmes, President of Echoblast. "By opening EBL-VA1, we're enabling companies in the region to build and deploy AI applications with the speed and economics that Echoblast is known for. We're seeing strong interest from Fortune 500 companies, government contractors, and research institutions who need compliant, performant GPU infrastructure."

The opening of EBL-VA1 follows Echoblast's successful IPO on NASDAQ in Q1 2030 under ticker EBLA. The company has continued to invest aggressively in infrastructure, talent, and product capabilities to maintain its market leadership in the competitive AI infrastructure space.

Echoblast plans to announce additional datacenter locations in 2027 as it executes its North American expansion roadmap. Current customers and prospects interested in accessing EBL-VA1 capacity are encouraged to contact Echoblast's sales team.

**About Echoblast, Inc.**

Echoblast is an AI neocloud platform that combines GPU rental with managed training and inference services. Founded in January 2025 and based in San Francisco, Echoblast empowers AI teams to build, train, and deploy large-scale machine learning models with unprecedented ease and cost efficiency. The company was part of Y Combinator's Winter 2025 cohort and went public on NASDAQ in Q1 2030. Learn more at echoblast.ai.

**Forward-Looking Statements**

This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Statements regarding planned datacenter expansions, customer demand, and future capacity are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially. Echoblast undertakes no obligation to update forward-looking statements except as required by law.
